heading: Incidents involving ships
label: 1906 Incidents involving ships
notes: Amendments1991— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section read as follows:Pub. L. 102–241“(a) As soon as he has knowledge of an incident, the master or other person in charge of a ship shall report it to the Secretary in the manner prescribed by Article 8 of the Convention.“(b) Upon receipt of the report of an incident involving a ship, other than one of United States registry or nationality or one operated under the authority of the United States, the Secretary shall take the action required by Article 8 of the Convention.”
amendments: Amendments1991— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section read as follows:Pub. L. 102–241“(a) As soon as he has knowledge of an incident, the master or other person in charge of a ship shall report it to the Secretary in the manner prescribed by Article 8 of the Convention.“(b) Upon receipt of the report of an incident involving a ship, other than one of United States registry or nationality or one operated under the authority of the United States, the Secretary shall take the action required by Article 8 of the Convention.”

children.heading: Requirement to report incident
children.label: a Requirement to report incident
children.text: The master, person in charge, owner, charterer, manager, or operator of a ship involved in an incident shall report the incident in the manner prescribed by Article 8 of the Convention in accordance with regulations promulgated by the Secretary for that purpose.

children.heading: Requirement to report discharge, probable discharge, or presence of oil
children.label: b Requirement to report discharge, probable discharge, or presence of oil

children.text: a ship of United States registry or nationality, or operated under the authority of the United States, wherever located;
another ship while in the navigable waters of the United States; or
a sea port or oil handling facility subject to the jurisdiction of the United States,
